# UC-19.1.43 · oVirt / RHV VDSM Host Reconnect Storm and Storage Lease Errors

## Description

VDSM logs capture host-to-engine reconnect loops, SCSI reservation failures, and storage lease problems that make VMs pause or fence. These signatures often appear before the Engine UI shows hosts as non-operational.

## Value

Surfaces host-side storage and lease faults so network or SAN teams can act before mass VM pauses.

## Implementation

Deploy UF file inputs on each KVM host for `vdsm.log`. Use `props.conf` to extract `level` and key oVirt tokens. Create a baseline for normal reconnect chatter; alert when error-weighted volume spikes per host. Join to host inventory for rack awareness.

## SPL

```spl
index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm" earliest=-2h
| search (lease OR "SAN lock" OR "storage domain" OR "Could not connect") AND (level="ERROR" OR level="WARN")
| stats count as events by host, storage_domain
| where events>=10
| sort -events
```

## Visualization

Table (hosts with lease errors), Timeline (VDSM errors), Choropleth by datacenter optional.

## References

- [Red Hat — Collecting vdsm logs (RHV troubleshooting)](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_virtualization/4.4/html/troubleshooting_guide/appe-vdsm_logs)
